facts.span: Blood cultures performed at admittance were positive for MSSA (SA1) in both samples taken after 18 hours

facts.content: SA1 strain susceptible to gentamicin, minocycline, tigecycline, rifampin, cotrimoxazol, vancomycin, teicoplanin, levofloxacin, ciprofloxacin, and linezolid

facts.content: SA1 vancomycin MIC 1 ug/mL by broth microdilution method
facts.span: The vancomycin MIC determined by the broth microdilution method was 1 ug/mL

facts.content: Nuclear MRI showed spondylodiscitis at L5-S1 with left iliopsoas muscle involvement
facts.span: Nuclear MRI of the spine showed spondylodiscitis at the L5-S1 level with left iliopsoas muscle involvement

facts.content: TEE showed vegetation and anterior mitral valve leaflet abscess causing mild mitral failure, interpreted as infective endocarditis with septic emboli to lumbosacral spine and left iliopsoas muscle
facts.span: a mass compatible with vegetation and an anterior mitral valve leaflet abscess causing mild mitral failure: this was interpreted as IE with septic emboli involving the lumbosacral spine and the left iliopsoas muscle

facts.content: Patient started on intravenous cephalothin for infective endocarditis (first admission)

facts.content: Beta-lactam rash with eosinophilia and interstitial nephritis caused switch from cephalothin to vancomycin
facts.span: For that reason, treatment was switched to intravenous vancomycin (1 g/12 hours)

facts.content: Patient received intravenous vancomycin during first admission (after cephalothin switch)
facts.span: treatment was switched to intravenous vancomycin (1 g/12 hours)

facts.span: a new blood culture was positive for MSSA (SA2) in both samples after 19.5 hours

facts.content: PCR detection of mecA gene was negative in both SA1 and SA2 isolates
facts.span: Polymerase chain reaction detection of the mecA gene was negative in both isolates

facts.content: SA1 vancomycin MIC 0.5 ug/mL and MBC 0.5 ug/mL at standard inoculum (10^5)
facts.span: The vancomycin MIC for SA1 and SA2 with the standard inoculum (10^5) was 0.5 and 1 ug/mL, respectively, and the minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) was 0.5 and 128 ug/mL, respectively

facts.content: SA2 vancomycin MIC 1 ug/mL and MBC 128 ug/mL at standard inoculum (10^5)
facts.span: The vancomycin MIC for SA1 and SA2 with the standard inoculum (10^5) was 0.5 and 1 ug/mL, respectively, and the minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) was 0.5 and 128 ug/mL, respectively

facts.content: SA1 and SA2 vancomycin MIC increased to 2 ug/mL and MBC to 512 ug/mL at higher inoculum (10^7)
facts.span: A higher inoculum (10^7) increased the MIC to 2 ug/mL and the MBC to 512 ug/mL in both isolates

facts.content: SA3 (vancomycin-stimulated) MIC and MBC at standard inoculum same as SA2; at higher inoculum (10^7) MIC increased to 4 ug/mL and MBC to 512 ug/mL
facts.span: After stimulation with increasing subinhibitory concentrations of vancomycin (SA3), the MIC and MBC with the standard inoculum were the same, whereas at a higher inoculum (10^7), the values increased to 4 ug/mL and 512 ug/mL for the MIC and MBC, respectively

facts.content: Population analysis showed SA2 colony development up to 3 ug/mL and SA3 up to 4 ug/mL, with 2-4 logs more growth between 1-4 ug/mL compared to SA1 which developed up to 2 ug/mL
facts.span: a development of colonies up to 3 and 4 ug/mL, respectively, was observed, with growth between 1 and 4 ug/mL, which was 2 to 4 logs more than in SA1, which developed up to 2 ug/mL

facts.content: SA2 classified as heterogeneous vancomycin-intermediate S. aureus (hVISA) with PAP-AUC ratio of 1.06 versus Mu3 strain
facts.span: SA2 and SA3 were identified as heterogeneous vancomycin-intermediate S. aureus (hVISA) on the basis of the population analysis profiling-area under the curve (PAP-AUC) ratios, showing PAP-AUC ratios of 1.06 and 1.26, respectively

facts.content: SA3 classified as hVISA with PAP-AUC ratio of 1.26 versus Mu3 strain
facts.span: SA2 and SA3 were identified as heterogeneous vancomycin-intermediate S. aureus (hVISA) on the basis of the population analysis profiling-area under the curve (PAP-AUC) ratios, showing PAP-AUC ratios of 1.06 and 1.26, respectively

facts.content: SA1 classified as vancomycin-susceptible with PAP-AUC ratio of 0.83 versus Mu3 strain
facts.span: SA1 was identified as vancomycin-susceptible, showing a PAP-AUC ratio of 0.83, compared to the AUC of the Mu3 strain

facts.content: Electron microscopy of SA2 showed a thickened cell wall
facts.span: Electron microscopy of SA2 showed a thickened cell wall

facts.content: PFGE showed SA1 and SA2 displayed indistinguishable electrophoretic patterns confirming clonal relationship
facts.span: The clonal relationship determined by pulsed-field gel electrophoresis showed that both S. aureus isolates displayed indistinguishable electrophoretic patterns

facts.content: Persistent bacteremia possibly due to metastatic infectious focus in left iliopsoas muscle and vertebrae
facts.span: This persistent bacteremia could be due to the presence of a metastatic infectious focus, such as that in the left iliopsoas muscle and the vertebrae

facts.content: Initial vancomycin monotherapy may have failed to sterilize the bone due to poor vancomycin bone penetration
facts.span: the initial monotherapy with this antibiotic combined with short-duration parenteral antimicrobial treatment may have failed to sterilize the bone

facts.content: Negative aspirate cultures from iliopsoas lesion cannot rule out metastatic infectious focus due to low sensitivity of procedure in antibiotic-pretreated patients
facts.span: Negative cultures resulting from the aspiration biopsy of the muscle lesion cannot rule out the presence of a metastatic infectious focus

facts.content: Vancomycin MIC of 1 ug/mL or greater in the isolate could justify treatment failure
facts.span: the isolate showed a vancomycin MIC of 1 ug/mL or greater, which could justify treatment failure

facts.content: Rifampin could have been effective in this patient because of bone involvement in the spine
facts.span: Rifampin could have been effective here due to the patient's bone involvement in the spine

facts.content: Vancomycin trough of 14.1 mg/L did not reach recommended 15-20 mg/L, suggesting subpopulations with reduced vancomycin susceptibility may have arisen during therapy contributing to treatment failure
facts.span: the vancomycin doses administered in our case did not reach the recommended trough serum levels of 15-20 mg/L (14.1 mg/L), it could be assumed that S. aureus subpopulations with reduced susceptibility to vancomycin might have arisen during therapy

facts.content: Infection finally resolved after vancomycin treatment likely because of combination with gentamicin and rifampin
facts.span: The infection finally resolved after vancomycin treatment, likely because of its combination with gentamicin and rifampin
